debug_info.goal:
case mk.cons
a : Type
bs : List a
n : a
ns : List a
ok :
  ((n :: ns).isEmpty = true → bs.isEmpty = true ∨ bs.length = 1) ∧
    (bs.isEmpty = true → (n :: ns).isEmpty = true ∨ (n :: ns).length = 1)
⊢ failed to pretty print expression (use 'set_option pp.rawOnError true' for raw representation)

failed_attempts:
cases bs with
      | nil =>
        cases ns
        · simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL, List.init]
        · have h := ok.2 rfl; cases h <;> contradiction
      | cons m ms => simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL, List.init]

have hr (sl : SymList a) : sl.reverse.fromSL = sl.fromSL.reverse := by cases sl; simp [SymList.reverse, SymList.fromSL]
      simp [SymList.init, hr, SymList.fromSL_tailSL]
      induction (n :: ns ++ bs.reverse) with | nil => rfl | cons x xs ih => cases xs <;> simp [List.init, ih]

simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.fromSL, SymList.fromSL_tailSL]
      cases bs with
      | nil =>
        simp at ok; have h := ok.2 rfl; cases ns; rfl; cases h
      | cons b bs' =>
        induction ns generalizing n bs' <;> simp [List.init, *]
simp [SymList.init, SymList.fromSL_reverse, SymList.fromSL_tailSL, List.reverse_tail_reverse]

have h_rev q : (SymList.reverse q).fromSL = (SymList.fromSL q).reverse := by cases q; simp [SymList.fromSL, SymList.reverse]
      rw [SymList.init, h_rev, SymList.fromSL_tailSL, h_rev]
      generalize (n :: ns ++ bs.reverse) = L
      induction L with
      | nil => rfl
      | cons x xs ih =>
        cases xs with
        | nil => rfl
        | cons y ys => simp [List.init, List.tail, List.reverse, ih]

simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.fromSL]
      cases bs with
      | nil =>
        cases ok.2 rfl with
        | inl h => contradiction
        | inr h => simp at h; simp [h, SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL, SymList.nil]
      | cons b bs' =>
        simp [SymList.tailSL, SymList.balance_fromSL, SymList.fromSL]
simp [SymList.init, SymList.tailSL_fromSL, SymList.reverse, SymList.fromSL, List.reverse_tail]

have h1 s : (SymList.fromSL s).reverse = (SymList.reverse s).fromSL := by
        cases s; simp [SymList.fromSL, SymList.reverse]
      rw [SymList.init, ← h1, SymList.tailSL_fromSL, ← h1]
      generalize (n :: ns ++ bs.reverse) = L
      induction L with | nil => rfl | cons x xs ih => cases xs <;> simp [List.init, *]
simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, tailSL_fromSL, SymList.fromSL, List.reverse_tail_reverse]

have hr s : s.reverse.fromSL = (s.fromSL).reverse := by cases s; simp [SymList.reverse, SymList.fromSL]
      have ht s : s.tailSL.fromSL = (s.fromSL).tail := by cases s; simp [SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL_balance, SymList.fromSL]; split <;> simp [SymList.fromSL_balance, SymList.fromSL]
      have hi L : List.init L = L.reverse.tail.reverse := by induction L with | nil => rfl | cons x xs ih => cases xs <;> simp [List.init, *]
      simp [SymList.init, hr, ht, hi, SymList.fromSL]
simp [SymList.init, SymList.fromSL_reverse, SymList.fromSL_tailSL, List.reverse_tail_reverse]

have h : ∀ sl : SymList a, sl.reverse.fromSL = sl.fromSL.reverse :=
        by intro ⟨as, bs, ok⟩; simp [SymList.fromSL, SymList.reverse]
      simp [SymList.init, h, SymList.fromSL_tailSL, List.reverse_tail, List.init]
simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL, SymList.balance_fromSL, List.init_eq_reverse_tail_reverse, *]

cases bs with
      | nil =>
        have h := ok.2 rfl; cases ns; rfl; cases h
      | cons m ms =>
        simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL_balance, SymList.fromSL, List.reverse_cons, ← List.append_assoc]
        generalize (n :: ns ++ ms.reverse) = l
        induction l with | nil => rfl | cons x xs ih => cases xs with | nil => rfl | cons y ys => simp [List.init, ih]

have h_init : ∀ {α} (L : List α) (x : α), List.init (L ++ [x]) = L
        | [], _ => rfl
        | y :: ys, x => by unfold List.init; cases ys <;> simp [h_init]
      have h_rev (sl : SymList a) : sl.reverse.fromSL = sl.fromSL.reverse :=
        by cases sl; simp [SymList.fromSL, SymList.reverse]
      cases bs with
      | nil =>
        have h := ok.2 (by rfl)
        cases ns with
        | nil => simp [SymList.init, h_rev, SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL]
        | cons m ms => simp [List.length] at h
      | cons b bs' =>
        simp [SymList.init, h_rev, SymList.tailSL, SymList.balance_fromSL, List.reverse_cons, ← List.append_assoc, h_init]

simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL]
      cases bs with
      | nil =>
        have h := ok.2 rfl
        simp at h
        cases h with
        | inl h1 => contradiction
        | inr h2 =>
          cases ns
          · simp [SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL, List.init, SymList.nil]
          · contradiction
      | cons b bs =>
        simp [SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL]
        have h (L : List a) (x : a) : List.init (x :: L ++ [b]) = x :: L := by
          induction L generalizing x with
          | nil => simp [List.init]
          | cons y ys ih => simp [List.init, ih]
        exact h (ns ++ bs.reverse) n

have h q : (SymList.tailSL q).fromSL = q.fromSL.tail := by
        cases q with | mk as bs ok =>
        cases as <;> simp [SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL, SymList.check, SymList.nil, *]
        cases bs <;> simp [*, SymList.fromSL, SymList.nil]; cases ok.1 rfl <;> simp [*]
      simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, h, SymList.fromSL]
      generalize (n :: ns ++ bs.reverse) = L
      induction L with
      | nil => simp [List.init]
      | cons x xs ih => cases xs <;> simp [List.init, ih]
induction ns generalizing bs <;> cases bs <;> simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL, List.init, *]

have h (l : List a) : l.init = l.reverse.tail.reverse := by
        induction l with | nil => rfl | cons x xs ih => cases xs <;> simp [List.init, List.reverse, List.tail, ih]
      rw [h, SymList.init]
      simp [SymList.reverse, SymList.fromSL, List.reverse_append]
      cases bs <;> simp [SymList.tailSL, SymList.rebalance, SymList.fromSL, List.tail, List.reverse_append]
      cases ns; rfl; cases ok.2 rfl <;> contradiction

cases bs with
      | nil =>
        simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL]
        cases ok.2 rfl <;> simp_all [List.length]
      | cons b bs' =>
        simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, SymList.balance, SymList.fromSL_balance, List.init_append]

have h {α} (L : List α) (x : α) : List.init (L ++ [x]) = L := by
        induction L <;> simp [List.init, *]
      cases bs with
      | nil =>
        simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL] at *
        cases ns with | nil => rfl | cons _ _ => cases ok.2 rfl
      | cons m ms =>
        simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL, List.init, h]

rw [SymList.init, SymList.fromSL_reverse, SymList.fromSL_tailSL, SymList.fromSL_reverse]
      generalize (n :: ns ++ bs.reverse) = l
      induction l with
      | nil => rfl
      | cons x xs ih => cases xs with | nil => rfl | cons y ys => simp [List.init, ih]

have h {α} (l : List α) (x : α) : (l ++ [x]).init = l := by induction l <;> simp [List.init, *]
      simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL, SymList.balance, SymList.nil, h]
      cases bs <;> simp_all [List.init]
      cases ok.2 (by rfl) with | inl h => contradiction | inr h => cases ns <;> simp_all [List.init]
simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.fromSL, SymList.fromSL_tailSL, ← List.reverse_tail, List.init]

cases bs with
      | nil =>
        have h := ok.2 rfl; cases ns with
        | nil => simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, SymList.nil, SymList.fromSL, List.init]
        | cons m ms => cases h with | inl h => contradiction | inr h => injection h
      | cons b bs' =>
        simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, fromSL_balance, SymList.fromSL]
        induction ns generalizing n with
        | nil => simp [List.init, List.reverse_append]
        | cons m ms ih => simp [List.init, ih]

cases bs with
      | nil =>
        have h := ok.2 rfl; cases h with | h1 | h2 => contradiction
        injection h2 with h; have h' := List.length_eq_zero.mp h; subst h'
        simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL, SymList.balance_fromSL, List.init]
      | cons b bs' =>
        simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL, SymList.balance_fromSL]
simp [SymList.init, SymList.fromSL_reverse, SymList.fromSL_tailSL, List.reverse_tail]
simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.fromSL, SymList.fromSL_tailSL, init_reverse_tail_reverse]

have h1 q : (SymList.reverse q).fromSL = q.fromSL.reverse := by cases q; simp [SymList.fromSL, SymList.reverse]
      have h2 q : (SymList.tailSL q).fromSL = q.fromSL.tail := by
        cases q; rename_i f b ok; cases f <;> (simp [SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL, SymList.balance]; cases b with | nil => rfl | cons x xs => cases xs; rfl; have := ok.1 rfl; cases this <;> simp at *)
      have h3 l : l.init = l.reverse.tail.reverse := by induction l <;> [simp, (rename_i x xs; cases xs <;> simp [*])]
      simp [SymList.init, h1, h2, SymList.fromSL] at *; split <;> simp [h3]
simp [SymList.init, SymList.fromSL_reverse, SymList.fromSL_tailSL, List.init_eq_reverse_tail_reverse]

simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL, SymList.balance, List.init]
      split <;> simp [SymList.fromSL_balance, *]
      intro h; have := ok.2 h; cases ns; rfl; simp at this
cases bs <;> simp [SymList.init, SymList.reverse, SymList.tailSL, SymList.fromSL, List.init, *]
